creator: cwf-ml@arcor.de
text:
syslog-ng is a replacement for the BSD's syslog daemon with a few nifty security features.

I just recently upgraded it to the current port because portaudit complained.

Now I suddenly got a Python installation nobody needs, plus a ridiculously overweight string conversion library. Plus Gnome stuff (gamin, gio-fam-backend) I really don't need either. All I wanted was a syslog daemon with better access control.

Checking the dependencies I notice that syslog-ng forces the installation of 2 full blown scripting environments (Perl AND Python), without ever using either of them. What's worse, it has them as run-dependencies (possibly via glib?), which means you cant remove them after installation.

Oh - and it also builds and installs icu, which on a 2.8G Xeon needs slightly less than 137 years to compile.

# [syslog-ng] pwd
/usr/ports/sysutils/syslog-ng

# [syslog-ng] make all-depends-list
/usr/ports/devel/gmake
/usr/ports/devel/pkg-config
/usr/ports/sysutils/eventlog
/usr/ports/devel/pcre
/usr/ports/devel/glib20
/usr/ports/devel/gio-fam-backend
/usr/ports/devel/gettext
/usr/ports/lang/perl5.10
/usr/ports/devel/libtool
/usr/ports/lang/python27
/usr/ports/devel/icu
/usr/ports/devel/gamin
/usr/ports/converters/libiconv

I have no idea who the actual culprit is - but the various involved maintainers might want to think hard about decrapifying their respective packages.

How-To-Repeat: install a naked system

install syslog-ng

marvel at the package list
